risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Constraining Implementation Capacity
risk_summary: Political and governance fragility in South Sudan is creating substantial constraints on project implementation, with the risk category rated as 'High' and persisting across multiple reporting periods. This affects the government's ability to execute project activities and maintain institutional continuity.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Category Rating]: Substantial [at Approval], High [Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 04], High [Proposed Rating]

risk_summary: Macroeconomic risks have escalated from 'Substantial' at project approval to 'High' in current ratings, indicating worsening economic conditions that threaten project implementation and sustainability in South Sudan's fragile context.
evidence_quote: Macroeconomic [Risk Category Rating]: Substantial [at Approval], High [Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 04], High [Proposed Rating]

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints Amid Governance Fragility
risk_summary: Institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability has deteriorated from 'Substantial' at approval to 'High' risk, reflecting challenges in government ministries' ability to execute project activities in a fragile governance environment.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability [Risk Category Rating]: Substantial [at Approval], High [Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 04], High [Proposed Rating]

risk_title: Refugee and Host Community Integration Amid Displacement Pressures
risk_summary: The project's expansion to include refugee and host community education (Component 3) reflects underlying displacement pressures in South Sudan. The development objective was modified to explicitly address refugee access to education, indicating this is an emerging FCV-related implementation challenge.
evidence_quote: Current Development Objective (Approved as part of Additional Financing package Seq. 1 on 11-Sep-2024): The Project's Development Objective is to increase skills development opportunities in teaching and digital agriculture, improve access to education for refugee and host community children and strengthen capacity for management of the education system.

risk_title: Delayed Implementation in Refugee-Hosting Areas Due to Operational Challenges
risk_summary: Component 3 activities targeting 200 schools in refugee-hosting areas remain in preparatory phases with no actual rehabilitation or re-operationalization commenced as of September 2025. This delay reflects implementation challenges in operating within fragile, displacement-affected contexts.
evidence_quote: MoGEI has completed the identification of the 200 schools that will benefit from this project activity, with contracting for rehabilitation works anticipated to commence within the next six months.
